slices.name: tenant-context-walking-skeleton
slices.description: Platform operator creates a tenant and authenticated requests carry tenant context through one protected admin endpoint.
slices.outcome: tenant identity exists and protected APIs can resolve tenant context

slices.name: tenant-admin-management-scope
slices.description: Tenant admin manages products and userbase records scoped to their tenant with audit logs.
slices.outcome: admin management data is tenant-filtered and auditable

slices.name: channel-catalog-capabilities
slices.description: Tenant admin creates channel definitions with provider, country/operator scope, and supported capabilities.
slices.outcome: tenant-owned channels can be selected by future campaigns and service routing

slices.name: channel-credential-binding
slices.description: Tenant admin binds a secret-backed credential reference to a channel without exposing secret material.
slices.outcome: channel routing can resolve active credential references securely

slices.name: tenant-campaign-binding
slices.description: Tenant admin creates and reads campaigns scoped by tenant and bound to a compatible channel.
slices.outcome: campaigns are tenant-scoped and channel-compatible before public use

slices.name: tenant-acquisition-flow
slices.description: End subscriber starts and confirms an acquisition transaction through a tenant campaign.
slices.outcome: transactions, consent, HE, attribution, and next action are tenant-scoped
failure_modes_to_cover: pending transaction reuse across tenant boundary

slices.name: channel-subscription-routing
slices.description: API-integrated partner and acquisition services route optin, confirm, optout, status, MT, and charge through tenant channel configuration.
slices.outcome: subscription-external never uses global TIMWE credentials when tenant/channel is required

slices.name: notification-and-cadence-routing
slices.description: Notifications, message outbox, cadence state, and worker dispatch preserve tenant and channel context.
slices.outcome: notification and cadence workflows are tenant-filtered and channel-aware

slices.name: postback-attribution-routing
slices.description: Ad partner postbacks and attribution rules resolve by tenant, campaign, channel, and provider.
slices.outcome: conversion postbacks are tenant/provider-specific and recoverable through tenant-scoped DLQ

slices.name: tenant-reporting-operations
slices.description: Tenant admin views reports, exports, queues, charging failures, renewal, and health filtered by tenant and channel.
slices.outcome: tenant operations can diagnose platform health without cross-tenant data leakage

slices.name: legacy-data-migration-isolation
slices.description: Platform operator migrates existing global data into a default tenant and verifies tenant isolation constraints.
slices.outcome: existing production data is backfilled with tenant_id and verified before multi-tenant enforcement

slices.name: public-tenant-routing
slices.description: Public landing, campaign, HE, callback, and gateway routes resolve tenant deterministically before work begins.
slices.outcome: public routes cannot choose the wrong tenant when campaign slugs overlap

slices.name: inbound-callback-correlation
slices.description: Inbound TIMWE/MNO/partner callbacks correlate to tenant and channel before updating subscription, notification, charge, or postback state.
slices.outcome: callbacks update only the intended tenant/channel state or quarantine safely

slices.name: admin-portal-tenant-workspace
slices.description: Admin portal exposes a tenant workspace, tenant route guards, platform operator tenant switching, and API tenant error handling.
slices.outcome: tenant admins see and mutate only their tenant resources in the UI

slices.name: platform-ops-secret-observability
slices.description: Platform operator removes unsafe secret patterns, documents rotation, and adds tenant/channel observability labels and dashboards.
slices.outcome: tenant channels operate with safe credentials and visible tenant-specific health

slices.name: partner-channel-onboarding-contracts
slices.description: Partner receives versioned tenant/channel API, callback, credential, sandbox, and postback contracts.
slices.outcome: new tenant channels can be onboarded without bespoke engineering discovery

slices.name: billing-charge-ownership
slices.description: Platform operator decides and proves the tenant-aware owner for charge and billing flows across subscription-external and the disabled billing service.
slices.entrypoint: subscription-external charge endpoints and services/billing transaction endpoints
slices.outcome: charge-capable channels do not split tenant billing state across ambiguous services

slices.name: tenant-claim-and-service-auth-contract
slices.description: Platform services share typed tenant claims, role scopes, trusted service headers, and request-context propagation.
slices.entrypoint: common/auth/auth0jwt, admin middleware, KrakenD trusted header policy
slices.outcome: all protected, public, partner, and worker flows rely on one tenant trust boundary

slices.name: tenant-asset-namespacing
slices.description: Campaign assets and presigned URLs use tenant-scoped object-storage keys and public-safe DTOs.
slices.outcome: landing pages cannot read or overwrite another tenant's campaign media

slices.name: tenant-observability-baseline
slices.description: HTTP services and workers emit safe tenant/channel context in logs, metrics, traces, and health signals.
slices.entrypoint: HTTP middleware logs, Prometheus metrics, worker health
slices.outcome: operators can diagnose tenant issues without high-cardinality or PII leakage

slices.name: Compose runtime schema bootstrap
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can start compose from an empty database and have runtime schema prerequisites applied before services poll or bootstrap admin schema.
slices.demo: Disposable PostgreSQL bootstrap plus notification/cadence/postback empty-poll query proof.

automated: disposable PostgreSQL bootstrap proof with scripts/compose-db-bootstrap.sh
disposable PostgreSQL notification/cadence/postback empty-poll query proof

slices.notes: Adds a one-shot db-bootstrap service and minimal runtime base SQL while preserving acquisition-api and subscription-external service-owned migrations as canonical.

slices.name: webspa-admin reproducible source checkout
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can verify webspa-admin from tracked superproject source without fetching an unavailable submodule commit.
slices.demo: Tracked package.json plus npm ci, npm run build, and ChromeHeadless 84/84 tests.
slices.notes: Replaces unreachable webspa-admin submodule gitlink with exact pinned local admin source to preserve tenant workspace functionality and make clean checkouts verifiable.

slices.name: Landing web dependency remediation
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can run landing-web dependency verification without Next/PostCSS advisories blocking release.
slices.demo: npm audit, Next build, and standalone runtime smoke returning HTTP 200 for /.

automated: standalone landing-web runtime smoke returns HTTP 200 for /

slices.notes: Upgrades landing-web to Next 16/React 19 and overrides transitive PostCSS to a patched version; updates required dynamic route params signatures for Next 16.

slices.name: Admin tenant account mapping
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can grant named admin principals all-tenant workspace access before database memberships exist.
slices.demo: Bootstrap admin emails resolve platform tenant workspaces; backend applies selected tenant only for platform-scoped identities.
slices.notes: Keeps Auth0 claims as the primary mapping interface, adds explicit verified-email bootstrap all-tenant admins for the requested emails, resolves selected tenant keys for reports, and gates selected tenant header enrichment on platform scope.

slices.name: Acquisition campaign slug migration startup fix
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can start acquisition-api against an existing database with legacy campaign slug foreign keys.
slices.demo: Repository migration test proves the tenant acquisition flow migration drops all legacy campaigns(slug) foreign keys before dropping campaigns_slug_key and does not use CASCADE.
slices.notes: Generalizes the previous hard-coded acquisition transaction FK drop into catalog-driven removal of all legacy foreign keys that reference public.campaigns(slug), allowing campaigns_slug_key to be removed for tenant-scoped duplicate slugs.

slices.name: Canonical nrg tenant migration
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can migrate tenantless production data to the canonical nrg tenant.
slices.demo: Migration tooling defaults to nrg, backfills tenant_id IS NULL rows, removes rollback-to-null, and webspa-admin bootstrap defaults use nrg.

automated: ! rg -n "legacy-default|LEGACY_TENANT|db-rollback-tenant-platform|--rollback|tenant_id = NULL|channel_id IS NULL|channel_id IS NOT NULL" scripts/db-migrate-tenant-platform.sh docs/tenant-platform-migration-runbook.md docs/tenant-channel-onboarding.md frontend/webspa-admin/src/environments services/acquisition-api/internal/transport/admin_test.go services/acquisition-api/internal/handler/reports_handler_test.go

slices.notes: Collapses the legacy-default tenant migration into canonical nrg ownership and removes active rollback-to-null tooling so tenantless production data no longer has an active compatibility path.

slices.name: Tenant catalog admin UI and API
slices.user_story: As an operator, I can list and update tenant catalog records from admin API and UI.
slices.demo: Operator-scoped admin can list tenants and patch name/status/default_country/metadata while tenant-scoped admins receive forbidden responses.
slices.notes: Adds operator-only tenant catalog list/update endpoints and a guarded webspa-admin tenant catalog route without adding parallel tenant management codepaths.

slices.name: Tenant nullable path enforcement
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can see which tenant nullable paths remain after the nrg migration.
slices.demo: The nullable-path audit classifies runtime, migration, script, and doc matches with prune criteria and emits proof/enforcement follow-up slices.

automated: rg -n "tenant_id IS NULL|tenant_id = NULL|WHERE .*tenant_id IS NULL|tenant_id UUID" services scripts docs frontend -g '!**/node_modules/**'

slices.notes: No NOT NULL migration was added. Active runtime nullable paths were classified for canonical collapse, while TMP-050 migration observability predicates remain until enforcement proof exists.

slices.name: Acquisition tenant nullable proof
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can prove acquisition/admin tenant-owned tables are ready for canonical enforcement.
slices.demo: Read-only row-count evidence proves or blocks acquisition/admin NOT NULL readiness.

automated: for v in DB_HOST DB_PORT DB_NAME DB_USER DB_PASSWORD PGHOST PGPORT PGDATABASE PGUSER PGPASSWORD DATABASE_URL; do ...; done
PGCONNECT_TIMEOUT=3 PGPASSWORD= psql -X -w -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -h localhost -p 5432 -U sm_admin -d subscription_manager -c 'SELECT 1;'
PGCONNECT_TIMEOUT=5 PGPASSWORD= psql -X -w -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -h 139.59.135.253 -p 5432 -U sm_admin -d subscription_manager -c 'SELECT 1;'

slices.notes: Completed with explicit credential blocker evidence: psql exists, but documented DB connection variables are unset and passwordless SELECT attempts fail with no password supplied.

slices.name: Subscription cadence tenant nullable proof
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can prove subscription/cadence tenant-owned tables are ready for canonical enforcement.
slices.demo: Read-only row-count evidence proves or blocks subscription/cadence NOT NULL readiness.
slices.notes: Completed with explicit credential blocker evidence: no .env file exists and documented APP_DATABASE_POSTGRESQL_*, PG_*, DB_*, and DATABASE_URL variables are unset.

slices.name: Tenant nullable runtime enforcement
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can remove active tenantless runtime ownership paths after proof exists.
slices.demo: Runtime code no longer accepts tenantless campaign/report/cadence matches and forward migrations clean legacy nullable lanes where proven safe.
slices.notes: Blocked on TMP-053 and TMP-054 proof so schema/runtime enforcement does not speculate against live data.

slices.name: Acquisition postback tenant routing bootstrap
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can start acquisition-api without postback dispatcher missing-column errors after admin bootstrap.
slices.demo: Startup schema bootstrap applies the acquisition-owned postback tenant-routing migration before dispatcher polling.
slices.notes: Follow-up to TMP-045 for service-local acquisition-api startup. Compose bootstrap already applied the canonical postback migrations; this slice aligns the in-process startup bootstrap.

slices.name: Add build/test CI pipeline (Angular, Next.js, Go lanes)
slices.user_story: As a platform engineer, I can open a PR and have Angular, Next.js, and Go service lanes run in parallel CI so regressions are caught before merge.
slices.demo: Push branch with only .github/workflows/ci.yml and CODEOWNERS; PR Actions tab shows ci.yml with all three lanes green.

manual_smoke: Open PR and confirm Actions tab shows ci.yml running Angular, Next.js, Go lanes in parallel
All lanes green from clean checkout

slices.notes: HIGH risk: 55 slices done with no CI gate — expect first-run failures. No root lockfile; run npm ci per-workspace. Chrome must be installed on runner for Karma headless. Go services have separate go.mod — matrix over services/.

slices.name: Add Sentry error monitoring to landing-web and webspa-admin
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can see runtime errors from landing-web and webspa-admin surfaced in Sentry with environment and release tags.
slices.demo: Trigger throw new Error(sentry-test) in each app (dev mode, test DSN); confirm event appears in Sentry with correct environment and release tag.

manual_smoke: Trigger manual throw new Error(sentry-test) in each app
Confirm event appears in Sentry with correct environment and release tag

slices.notes: Depends on TIMWE-CI-001 for SENTRY_RELEASE injection via ${{ github.sha }}. Go service Sentry integration and source-map upload are follow-up slices. Production DSN rotation out of scope.

slices.name: careerify-tenant-onboarding-seed
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I can onboard the careerify tenant with its web-gh-airteltigo channel and provider credentials so downstream gateway and notification slices route to real tenant rows.
slices.demo: Run the new migration locally; SELECT tenant_id FROM tenants WHERE tenant_key='careerify' returns a UUID; tenant_channels has web-gh-airteltigo bound to that tenant; tenant_routing.go:217 query for that pair returns provider credentials.

automated: psql $DATABASE_URL -c "SELECT tenant_id FROM tenants WHERE tenant_key='careerify'" | grep -E '[0-9a-f]{8}'
psql $DATABASE_URL -c "SELECT channel_id FROM tenant_channels WHERE channel_key='web-gh-airteltigo'" | grep -E '[0-9a-f]{8}'

slices.notes: Data prerequisite for TMP-067/068/069. Migration must also populate provider credentials referenced by tenant_routing.go:217. Use existing seed pattern from earlier nrg onboarding.

slices.name: krakend-notification-tenant-propagation
slices.user_story: As an MNO partner, my callback to /api/v1/notification/{type}/{partnerRole}?tenant_key=careerify&channel_key=web-gh-airteltigo reaches the notification service with X-Tenant-Key and X-Channel-Key injected by KrakenD so tenant context is resolved before any work.
slices.demo: curl through gateway against 6 notification URLs (mo, mt/dn, user-optin, user-renewed, user-optout, charge) for careerify; notification service log shows tenant_id resolved for careerify and request body is persisted scoped to that tenant.

manual_smoke: curl -X POST 'http://api.localhost/api/v1/notification/mo/2117?tenant_key=careerify&channel_key=web-gh-airteltigo' -d '{}' and confirm notification service logs tenant_id.

slices.notes: Touch krakend/krakend.json (and templates if templated). Add input_query_strings for tenant_key/channel_key and use header injection (KrakenD modifier or static plugin) to set X-Tenant-Key / X-Channel-Key on upstream request. No notification service code change required if upstream resolves headers via existing tenantctx; verify.

slices.name: krakend-subscription-tenant-path-routing
slices.user_story: As an external subscription partner, POST to /api/external/v1/{tenant_key}/{channel_key}/subscriptions/{optin|confirm|optout|status} reaches the existing subscription-external admin handlers with tenant and channel resolved by KrakenD header injection (Option A), with zero subscription-external handler changes.
slices.demo: curl POST through gateway for careerify/web-gh-airteltigo/subscriptions/optin returns 2xx; subscription-external log shows tenant_id and channel_id resolved; admin_actions row written with correct tenant scoping.

manual_smoke: curl -X POST 'http://api.localhost/api/external/v1/careerify/web-gh-airteltigo/subscriptions/optin' -d '{...}' returns 2xx and resolves tenant.

slices.notes: Four new KrakenD endpoints (optin, confirm, optout, status). Each rewrites path to subscription-external admin handler at /api/v1/subscription-external/admin/{op} and injects X-Tenant-Key / X-Channel-Key from path captures. No subscription-external code change expected; existing partner_handler.go:262-293 already resolves headers.

slices.name: tenant-resolver-header-query-precedence
slices.user_story: As an integration engineer, the canonical tenant resolver in common/auth/tenantctx enforces deterministic precedence between header and query sources of tenant_key/channel_key, refuses conflicting pairs, and is documented so backends and gateway authors share one rule.
slices.demo: Unit tests in common/auth/tenantctx assert: header wins when both header and query agree; conflict between header and query returns refusal; query alone is accepted only when header absent and request comes through gateway trust boundary; mixed-case keys normalized.
slices.notes: Defence-in-depth slice. Without this, TMP-067 and TMP-068 still work as long as KrakenD config is correct, but a gateway misconfig (query lost or wrong header name) would silently misroute. This slice makes the contract explicit + tested. Documents the rule in docs/tenant-channel-onboarding.md or a sibling doc.

slices.name: careerify-tenant-e2e-smoke
slices.user_story: As a platform operator, I run a smoke matrix that exercises the 10 inbound URLs (6 notification + 4 subscription) for careerify+web-gh-airteltigo through nginx -> KrakenD -> backend, confirms tenant scoping end-to-end, and verifies cross-tenant injection attempts are rejected.
slices.demo: scripts/smoke/careerify-tenant-e2e.sh runs all 10 sample URLs, captures logs proving tenant_id=careerify on each, executes 3 cross-tenant injection cases (mismatched header vs query, foreign tenant key, missing channel), and prints a green pass matrix.

manual_smoke: Operator runs the smoke matrix against staging and attaches output to value-gate-report.md.

slices.notes: Closing slice. Produces the artifact set required to declare careerify onboarding shippable. Failure here bounces back to whichever upstream slice owns the gap.

slices.name: notification-tenant-enforcement
slices.user_story: As a security reviewer, an MNO callback to /api/v1/notification/{type}/{partnerRole} is rejected with HTTP 4xx when tenant_key resolves to an unknown row or channel_key is missing, so unknown tenants cannot quietly persist notifications under NULL tenant context.
slices.demo: POST /api/v1/notification/mo/2117?tenant_key=evil-tenant returns 4xx; POST /api/v1/notification/mo/2117?tenant_key=careerify (no channel_key) returns 4xx; happy path /...?tenant_key=careerify&channel_key=web-gh-airteltigo still returns 200; refusal smoke Cases B and C now pass.
slices.notes: Follow-up from TMP-070 smoke: Cases B and C currently HTTP 200. Split tenantIDForAdminRead into present-but-unknown vs absent. See spec.md for details.

slices.name: subscription-gateway-trusted-auth
slices.user_story: As an external subscription partner, POST to /api/external/v1/{tenant_key}/{channel_key}/subscriptions/{op} reaches subscription-external and is processed under the correct tenant, instead of HTTP 400 trusted service secret is not configured.
slices.demo: Through-gateway curl to 4 subscription endpoints for careerify returns 2xx; cross-tenant refusal Case A returns 409 (not 400); subscription-external audit log shows tenant_id and channel_id.
slices.notes: Follow-up from TMP-070 smoke: admin handlers reject for missing trusted-service identity. Spec defines two options (A) KrakenD mints HMAC identity, (B) route to non-trusted external handlers. Pick one in spec phase.

slices.name: krakend-fc-template-parity
slices.user_story: As a release engineer, the KrakenD container built from docker-compose serves the same endpoints as krakend/krakend.json so TMP-067/068 tenant routing works in production, not only when KrakenD is run directly against the static config.
slices.demo: Render FC template via krakend check -dc; diff against krakend/krakend.json shows the 10 new endpoints rendered; docker-compose up krakend serves them; smoke matrix runs green against compose-rendered config.

manual_smoke: docker-compose up -d krakend; HOST=http://127.0.0.1:8090 bash scripts/smoke/careerify-tenant-e2e.sh

slices.notes: Follow-up from TMP-070 smoke: krakend.json edits are not in krakend/config/templates/. Production runtime path uses FC; static-config edits do not reach it.
